hotham.narrative: This is worth coming back for!  We've had an average of 17 cm of cold snow overnight and there is still the odd burst being blown through by the strong northwest wind this morning.  The current temperature is minus 4, and the forecast is calling for the snow showers to intensify later in the day. This snow has certainly improved the cover below the road and I'm hopeful that we'll be able to get The Village Chair running today.  An assessment will be made at first light, and if it's a goer, the operations teams will be doing what they can to get it spinning as soon as possible.  And I suspect the strong wind may play havoc with the upper chair lifts, so keep a close eye on the lift status this morning.    I'll hopefully see you cutting a few laps of The Village Loop in good snow conditions today, but regardless of how things play out in the short term, we are definitely heading in the right direction.  Enjoy. Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: This is worth coming back for!  We've had an average of 17 cm of cold snow overnight and there is still the odd burst being blown through by the strong northwest wind this morning.  The current temperature is minus 4, and the forecast is calling for the snow showers to intensify later in the day. This snow has certainly improved the cover below the road and I'm hopeful that we'll be able to get The Village Chair running today.  An assessment will be made at first light, and if it's a goer, the operations teams will be doing what they can to get it spinning as soon as possible.  And I suspect the strong wind may play havoc with the upper chair lifts, so keep a close eye on the lift status this morning.    I'll hopefully see you cutting a few laps of The Village Loop in good snow conditions today, but regardless of how things play out in the short term, we are definitely heading in the right direction.  Enjoy. Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: It was awesome to see so many people loving the laps of the Village Chair yesterday, and there'll be more fun to be had out there today.  The fun may be a little damper than ideal, but the turns will be soft and forgiving. We've had another 8 cm in the past 24 hours, however it warmed overnight and the precipitation is very borderline as I write this.  The current temp is right on zero, (the forecast top for the day), and the wind is a strong northwesterly which may increase further during the morning.  The wind may impact the upper lift operations again today, so keep an eye on the status of your preferred lifts.   Rug up in your most weatherproof garments and get out there for some exhilerating laps! Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: It's another morning of soft and forgiving turns for the resilient.  We've had another 1 cm of wet snow overnight, and there is more borderline precipitation coming down as I write.  The current temp is just above zero (heading for a top of 1 degrees), the wind has backed off a notch but is still a stiff northwesterly, making the wind-chill about minus 10 up the top. Don't let a little rugged mountain weather put you off, as the turns will be fun out there today.  The wind may have dropped enough to open another lift or two above the road, but be sure to don your good gear and check the lift status before heading out there.  And if storm riding isn't your thing, it looks like we'll have plenty of great weather days once things clear up overnight.  Village is scheduled to open at 7.30 this morning.  Enjoy the Ride! Cheers Bill

Don't let a little rugged mountain weather put you off, as the turns will be fun out there today.  The wind may have dropped enough to open another lift or two above the road, but be sure to don your good gear and check the lift status before heading out there.  And if storm riding isn't your thing, it looks like we'll have plenty of great weather days once things clear up overnight.  Village is scheduled to open at 7.30am this morning.  Enjoy the ride! Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: After the final snow flurries yesterday afternoon the storm has cleared out, the sky is filled with stars and a there's a stunning sunrise on the way. At 6am the temp is bang on zero after an overnight low of -3 and it should max out at a balmy 6 degrees. Right now the wind is a strong southerly bringing the windchill down, making it feel much colder in exposed areas. However this is expected to ease as the day progresses. With the clearing skies and drop in temps the snowmakers were able to fire up and they're  still going now adding some texture to the cover. The surface feels like it's been turned over well, however just properly assess it as it could be firm in places also. It was awesome to get Road Runner up and running yesterday and the same lifts are ready to roll today. If you can't get enough during the day head down to the Big D tonight where the good times will roll on under lights until 8:30pm. Have a fun one. Cheers, Burner.

hotham.narrative: If you prefer the suns rays I think you'll enjoy the foreseeable future. I can see stars everywhere, there's an alpenglow developing out to the east and we have another bright sunny day on the way. The current temp is +1.5 and could climb to as high as +7 degrees later on. In the Village right now there's a moderate wind out of the SSE. Same lifts will be on the spin today with the Summit Quad loading from 7:30am for the early birds. The open groomed terrain will definitely be the best bet. Even though the temps didn't get super low last night the surface will start out firm with sunny aspects softening as the day warms. Slap on the sunscreen and enjoy! Cheers, Burner.

hotham.narrative: It'll be another beaut sunny day on the hill.  The sky is full of stars and there is just a puff of a breeze from the east.  The current temp is a warm 4.8 degrees and we are heading for a balmy top of 8 degrees.   Although the temp is warm, the humidity has remained very low since yesterday afternoon, which has allowed the snowmakers to have a good run overnight and they are still pumping it out down in the valley this morning.  This should make for pretty good turns on firm groomers this morning, and the sunny aspects should soften as the day progresses.  Enjoy.   Heavenly Valley Lift is closed and the area is Not Recommended.   The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.  The conditions are only suitable for Advanced and Expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.  The Extreme Area is Closed due to an extreme ice hazard.  Do Not Enter! Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: It'll be another glorious sunny and calm day on the mountain.  The sky is clear, the current temp is 6.4 degrees, there is no wind, and we are heading for a very warm top temp of 9 degrees.  Although it's been warm overnight, the snow has had a low humidity freeze, and it will be firm first thing before the sunny aspects start to soften for fun spring-like conditions. The Summit will open at 7.30am this morning, followed by all other scheduled lifts at 8.30am. Night Skiing at the Big D is on until 8.30pm tonight.  Heavenly Valley Lift is closed and the area is not recommended. The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.  The conditions are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.  The Extreme Area is closed due to an extreme ice hazard.  Do not enter! Slap on the screen and have a beaut day on the hill.  Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: It'll be another glorious sunny and calm day on the mountain.  The sky is clear, the current temp is 6.4 degrees, there is no wind, and we are heading for a very warm top temp of 9 degrees.  Although it's been warm overnight, the snow has had a low humidity freeze, and it will be firm first thing before the sunny aspects start to soften for fun spring-like conditions. The Summit will open at 7.30am this morning, followed by all other scheduled lifts at 8.30am. Night Skiing at the Big D is on until 8.30pm tonight.  Heavenly Valley Lift is closed and the area is not recommended. The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.  The conditions are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.  The Extreme Area is closed due to an extreme ice hazard.  Do not enter! Slap on the screen and have a beaut day on the hill.  Cheers Bill

The cover is marginal and the surface will remain icy all day on the shaded aspects.  The conditions are only suitable for advanced and expert skiers and riders, so think about your group's ability before traversing across from the Summit or Road Runner.  The Extreme Area is closed due to an extreme ice hazard.  Do not enter! Slap on the screen and have another beaut day on the hill. Cheers Bill

hotham.narrative: It'll be yet another gloriously sunny and mostly calm day on the mountain.  The sky is clear, the current temp is a balmy 7.4 degrees, there is no wind, and we are heading for another very warm top temp of 9 degrees.  Although it's been warm again overnight, the snow has had another low humidity freeze and will be firm first thing before the sunny aspects start to soften for fun spring-like conditions.  Shaded aspects will stay hard and slick all day.   The Summit will open at 7.30am this morning, followed by all other scheduled lifts at 9am. hotham.narrative: The extended run of stellar weather continues. At 6am I can see stars everywhere and the forecast is calling for another ripper day in the mountains. With another temperature inversion last night, the current temp is +4.3 and could max out at +6 degrees later on. The wind has picked up a few notches and is currently moderate to strong out of the North. Even though temps stayed above zero all night, the low humidity meant the snowmakers were able to get a window and they're still firing as I type. Just a reminder that as of today, scheduled lifts will revert back to regular operating hours - with the first lifts spinning from 8:30am.  The open groomed terrain will definitely be the best bet starting off firm with sunny aspects softening as the day warms. The cover on Heavenly Valley is marginal with shaded aspects likely to remain icy all day. Suitable for advanced and expert skiers only - if you venture out, be sure to ride with caution and observe all signage. The Extreme Area remains closed due do an extreme ice hazard. Do not enter!  Get out there for another fun one! Cheers, Burner

hotham.narrative: It'll be another cracker of a day on the hill!  The stars are shining and the snowguns are pumping it out thanks to the very low humidity all night.  The current temp is 2.7 degrees, heading for a top of 5 degrees and the wind is a moderate northerly which may increase later in the day.   I expect the snowmaking will make for good turns on the groomers this morning before some zones get scraped back to the hard base underneath.  The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring like turns as the day warms but the shaded and south facing slopes will probably stay very firm and slick for the day.   Heavenly Valley lift will be spinning once the patrol have completed their assessments, but the area is ONLY suitable for ADVANCED and EXPERT skiers and riders.  The cover is thin and the surface will likely remain icy on most runs for the day. The Extreme Area is Closed for ice hazard.  And DO NOT ATTEMPT to retrieve dropped equipment from any closed or out of bounds areas.  There is a dangerous sliding hazard out there at the moment. Slap on the screen, stick to the open areas and have a beaut day in the sun.  Cheers Bill

warnings: No run-level active snowmaking flags; this does not assert the plant was off or that the feed is comprehensive.

perisher.narrative: 15cm overnight and more on the way!. The snowstorm arrived at Perisher yesterday afternoon and has not stopped! Since yesterday,15cm of snow has fallen and more is expected today. Check out the snowstake to see it for yourself! In addition to the natural snowfall, the snow guns were also on last night to lend a helping hand, running from 3:00am and still going at time of writing. Today at Perisher is forecast to be cloudy with more snow showers. Winds are picking up from yesterday, with north westerly winds travelling between 33-40km/h with stronger gusts expected. Stronger winds may impact lift operations today, so be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates. Yesterday we saw more lifts and terrain open, including the Summit Quad Chair, bringing our current lift offering to 19 lifts. We'll continue to open more terrain as soon as it's possible and when it's safe to do so... stay tuned! Welcome to all of our guests competing in The Scots Race today - we wish you the best of luck in your races! To our other guests, please note that The Scots Race will take place on Front Valley, so it might be a good day to check out areas like Smiggin Holes. Off the slopes today, Jax Bar will see Jack Billman and Vibe Duo take the stage at 11:00am and 3:00pm respectively. Be sure to catch some of the performances in between runs! The forecast is calling for more snow, so be sure to check the snow cams throughout the day and come back for tomorrow's morning snow report!

perisher.narrative: Blizzard mode activated!. The snowstorm continues at Perisher, dropping another 10cm of snow since yesterday to bring the snowstorm total to 25cm so far! See the snowstake here . The blizzard like conditions that we saw yesterday will continue today with a top of 1 degrees, scattered snow showers, and strong winds. WNW winds are forecast to reach up to 43km/h with even stronger gusts, so be sure to rug up to avoid feeling that windchill. Over the weekend, the Summit Quad Chair opened for the first time this season, as well as the Hume T-Bar at Smiggins . The team will continue to open more lifts and terrain as soon as conditions allow. Please note that high winds may impact lift operations again today, so be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates. Looking ahead, there is still more snow in the forecast for the coming days, so let's see what else Mother Nature delivers!

perisher.narrative: 5cm to end the storm and a clear day ahead!. What should be the last of the storm rolled through last night, and we had another 5cm overnight, bringing the storm total to 33cm. Temps dropped to -3 degrees and the snowguns fired up at around 11pm and are still running this morning to build on the natural snowfall. The forecast is for a sunny day , with a top of 7 degrees and winds southwesterly 35 to 55 km/h, tending west to southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h in the early afternoon. We're expecting 21 lifts to spin today , and the wind hold of the last few days should have passed. The groomers will be firm and fast, and with the variable storm temperatures followed by a clear night, I'd expect off-piste to have set up, so recommend waiting for it to soften on the sunny aspects before getting too adventurous. We have another day of Sydney Interschools on Front Valley and Leichhardt - good luck to everyone competing. Smiggin Holes fireworks are also on tonight at 8pm. Mountain Ops will be looking to open more lifts and terrain as soon as it's safe to do so. As always, be the first to know by downloading the Perisher App , and keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates as lifts may open throughout the day.
warnings: snow_guns is treated as an operating count from current report context; retain raw field and re-check upstream semantics if its meaning changes.
No run-level active snowmaking flags; this does not assert the plant was off or that the feed is comprehensive.
Lift feed has no snowmaking field; status is unavailable, not inactive.

perisher.narrative: 5cm to end the storm and a clear day ahead!. What should be the last of the storm rolled through last night, and we had another 5cm overnight, bringing the storm total to 33cm. Temps dropped to -3 degrees and the snowguns fired up at around 11pm and are still running this morning to build on the natural snowfall. The forecast is for a sunny day , with a top of 7 degrees and winds southwesterly 35 to 55 km/h, tending west to southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h in the early afternoon. We're expecting 21 lifts to spin today , and the wind hold of the last few days should have passed. The groomers will be firm and fast, and with the variable storm temperatures followed by a clear night, I'd expect off-piste to have set up, so recommend waiting for it to soften on the sunny aspects before getting too adventurous. We have another day of Sydney Interschools on Front Valley and Leichhardt - good luck to everyone competing. Smiggin Holes fireworks are also on tonight at 8pm. Mountain Ops will be looking to open more lifts and terrain as soon as it's safe to do so. As always, be the first to know by downloading the Perisher App , and keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates as lifts may open throughout the day.

perisher.narrative: Mt Perisher 6 is Open, plus lifts in all four resort areas spinning!. Good morning and happy Thursday from Perisher. We are in for another stellar day on the mountain with clear skies and light winds. The forecast is calling for a top of 8 degrees, with winds southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h tending south to southeasterly 15 to 25 km/h in the morning. With temperatures dropping to below zero and low humidity, the snowguns fired up at around 5pm and are still running this morning. With MtP6 opening yesterday afternoon, alongside Ridge Chair in Blue Cow and the Blue Calf T-Bar in Guthega, we are expecting 26 lifts to spin today . Conditions will be firm and fast again today, with man-made snow topping up the base. We have 30 groomed runs across the resort. We'd recommend waiting for sunny aspects off-piste to soften before getting too adventurous. The Mountain Operations team are working hard to open more lifts and terrain as soon as it's safe to do so. Keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates, and make sure you've got notifications turned on in the Perisher App , we'll ping you the moment major lifts come online. A few good things to know: when a lift is opening for the first time this season, our lift crews and ski patrol need time to prep the lift and terrain, and run full safety checks before anyone can ride. It's all in the name of keeping the mountain safe for everyone, so please respect run closed signs and give the team the space to get it done properly. Congratulations to everyone who has competed in Sydney Interschools over the past couple of days, we're into the third day with racing on Front Valley and Leichhardt again. For entertainment , we've got live music at Jax Bar from midday today and from 1-3pm at Blue Cow Bistro.

The forecast is calling for a top of 8 degrees, with winds southwesterly 20 to 30 km/h tending south to southeasterly 15 to 25 km/h in the morning. With temperatures dropping to below zero and low humidity, the snowguns fired up at around 5pm and are still running this morning. With MtP6 opening yesterday afternoon, alongside Ridge Chair in Blue Cow and the Blue Calf T-Bar in Guthega, we are expecting 26 lifts to spin today . Conditions will be firm and fast again today, with man-made snow topping up the base. We have 30 groomed runs across the resort. We'd recommend waiting for sunny aspects off-piste to soften before getting too adventurous. The Mountain Operations team are working hard to open more lifts and terrain as soon as it's safe to do so. Keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates, and make sure you've got notifications turned on in the Perisher App , we'll ping you the moment major lifts come online. A few good things to know: when a lift is opening for the first time this season, our lift crews and ski patrol need time to prep the lift and terrain, and run full safety checks before anyone can ride. It's all in the name of keeping the mountain safe for everyone, so please respect run closed signs and give the team the space to get it done properly. Congratulations to everyone who has competed in Sydney Interschools over the past couple of days, we're into the third day with racing on Front Valley and Leichhardt again. For entertainment , we've got live music at Jax Bar from midday today and from 1-3pm at Blue Cow Bistro.
warnings: snow_guns is treated as an operating count from current report context; retain raw field and re-check upstream semantics if its meaning changes.
No run-level active snowmaking flags; this does not assert the plant was off or that the feed is comprehensive.
Lift feed has no snowmaking field; status is unavailable, not inactive.

perisher.narrative: Sunny laps on repeat!. We're set for another bluebird day here at Perisher. It's currently -2 degrees in the Perisher Valley and we're headed for a top of 9 degrees. Winds will be a light south to southwesterly, 15 to 20km/h. While it wasn't a super cold night, the low humidity still gave the snowguns another really solid run, with 200 guns firing across the resort. Yesterday, Lawson T-Bar opened so we have 27 lifts scheduled to run . It will be another firm and fast day on the groomers with more man-made snow on top. There are 32 groomed runs across the resort. The Mountain Operations team continue to work their magic, and we may have some more lifts come online over the coming days. As always, keep an eye on the lift status report for real-time updates, and make sure you've got notifications turned on in the Perisher App , so you get notified as soon as any new major lifts open. Today is the last day of Sydney Interschools with racing again on Front Valley and Leichhardt - most of the races should be finished by the early afternoon. We also have Volkl, Atomic and Black Crows demoing all the latest gear, so go and say hi to the crew between the Perisher Centre and the Skitube bridge. For entertainment , we've got live music at Blue Cow from 11:30am, Jax from 2:30pm, and Mid Station from 12pm bringing the good vibes to the mountain. Enjoy the sunshine and have a great day!

perisher.narrative: Bluebird skies and 32 lifts spinning!. We're in for a sunny Saturday at Perisher with a top of 11 degrees and light winds up to 13km/h, making for another lovely day for bluebird laps! It's currently a crisp -3 degrees, and last night's low humidity allowed 193 snow guns to fire up around the resort. Running since 5:30pm and still going at time of writing, the guns are topping up the base with man-made snow. Expect firm, fast conditions on the groomers today as a result. A number of new lifts opened yesterday, including Brumby T-Bar, Pleasant Valley Quad Chair, Eyre T-Bar and the Perisher Quad Express to the very top, unlocking more terrain for skiers and boarders. That brings the total number of lifts scheduled to run today to 32. The Mountain Operations team are working hard to open terrain as quickly and safely as possible. Be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates. Demo days are on at Perisher, with Atomic, Vandouml;lkl and Black Crows set up at Front Valley, showcasing all the latest gear for you to try! Some of Perisher's best skiers and riders will take to the rails today to showcase their top tricks in the Perisher Parks Showdown. As a result, the lower section of the Slopestyle Terrain Park will be closed to the public from 9:00am - 4:00pm. Catch a glimpse of the action while on the V8 if you can! Off the slopes, there's plenty of live music around the resort, including SAIJE performing on the deck at Blue Cow, and DJ Bolta bringing the vibes at Mid-Station. The Smith Street Band are also playing at The Station tonight, so grab your tickets if you haven't already. As the sun goes down, the fun continues at Perisher with night skiing and boarding from 6:00pm to 8:30pm. Guests can ski and ride under the stars, capped off with a special fireworks display (weather dependent). NSW Fire and Rescue will also be down at the bottom of the V8, so be sure to pay them a visit if you're out and about this evening!

perisher.narrative: Bluebird skies and 32 lifts spinning!. We're in for a sunny Saturday at Perisher with a top of 11 degrees and light winds up to 13km/h, making for another lovely day for bluebird laps! It's currently a crisp -3 degrees, and last night's low humidity allowed 193 snow guns to fire up around the resort. Running since 5:30pm and still going at time of writing, the guns are topping up the base with man-made snow. Expect firm, fast conditions on the groomers today as a result. A number of new lifts opened yesterday, including Brumby T-Bar, Pleasant Valley Quad Chair, Eyre T-Bar and the Perisher Quad Express to the very top, unlocking more terrain for skiers and boarders. That brings the total number of lifts scheduled to run today to 32. The Mountain Operations team are working hard to open terrain as quickly and safely as possible. Be sure to download the Perisher App or keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates. Demo days are on at Perisher, with Atomic, Vandouml;lkl and Black Crows set up at Front Valley, showcasing all the latest gear for you to try! Some of Perisher's best skiers and riders will take to the rails today to showcase their top tricks in the Perisher Parks Showdown. As a result, the lower section of the Slopestyle Terrain Park will be closed to the public from 9:00am - 4:00pm. Catch a glimpse of the action while on the V8 if you can! Off the slopes, there's plenty of live music around the resort, including SAIJE performing on the deck at Blue Cow, and DJ Bolta bringing the vibes at Mid-Station. The Smith Street Band are also playing at The Station tonight, so grab your tickets if you haven't already. As the sun goes down, the fun continues at Perisher with night skiing and boarding from 6:00pm to 8:30pm. Guests can ski and ride under the stars, capped off with a special fireworks display (weather dependent). NSW Fire and Rescue will also be down at the bottom of the V8, so be sure to pay them a visit if you're out and about this evening!

perisher.narrative: Sunny laps for Sunday!. The sunshine continues at Perisher, with another clear day and a top of 11 degrees. Winds stay gentle, blowing north-easterly at around 12km/h. Low overnight humidity meant we could fire up the snow guns again, with 199 guns running across the resort from 6:00pm and still going this morning. With man-made snow topping up the groomers, conditions will again be firm and fast. After a string of new lift openings earlier in the week, plus yesterday's opening of the Home Rope Tow, we now have 32 lifts open and scheduled to spin today. As always, check the lift report for real-time updates on lift operations, or download the Perisher App . In between runs, catch some live music around the resort. Indie folk duo SAIJE return to Jax Bar, while The Wildfires head up to Blue Cow, bending the boundaries of country, blues and soul. Enjoy the sunny laps!

With man-made snow topping up the groomers, conditions will again be firm and fast. After a string of new lift openings earlier in the week, plus yesterday's opening of the Home Rope Tow, we now have 32 lifts open and scheduled to spin today. As always, check the lift report for real-time updates on lift operations, or download the Perisher App . In between runs, catch some live music around the resort. Indie folk duo SAIJE return to Jax Bar, while The Wildfires head up to Blue Cow, bending the boundaries of country, blues and soul. Enjoy the sunny laps!

perisher.narrative: A sunny start to the week!. It's a mostly sunny Monday at Perisher, with a top of 10 degrees and light westerly winds between 13-16km/h. We have 29 lifts scheduled to spin across the resort today - keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates on lift operations or download the Perisher App to stay in the loop. Demo days are in full swing at Perisher, with some great brands in-resort showcasing the latest ski and snowboard gear, including Burton, Nitro, Jones, Rossignol, Head and Elan. If you're on the hunt for your next board or set of skis, pop over and see them at Front Valley, between the Skitube bridge and Perisher Centre. There's plenty more to look forward to this week too, with further demo days, live music, Station gigs and Christmas in July all on the cards. Head to the Events page or follow Perisher's socials to stay across it all.

perisher.narrative: Make the most of today’s sunshine!. Tuesday's shaping up as another mostly sunny day at Perisher, with the temperature reaching a high of 9 degrees and light westerly winds topping out at 17km/h. Although temperatures are running slightly above normal, low humidity overnight let our snowmaking team get to work, with 89 guns running through the early evening to top up the base. Expect the same firm, fast conditions underfoot as yesterday. 29 lifts are expected to spin again today, but as always, keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates, or download the Perisher App to stay in the loop. A warm welcome to all our guests competing in the ACT and Regional NSW Interschools this week! To our other guests: please note racing will be taking place on Front and Centre Valley, so it might be worth starting your days at other resort areas like Smiggins or Blue Cow. The Ski and Board Demo Days continue on Front Valley, with Burton, Nitro, Jones, Rossignol, Head, and Elan all in-resort showcasing their latest gear! If you're in the market for new equipment, be sure to say hello and put it to the test! Night Skiing and Boarding is also on tonight from 6:00-8:30pm, offering a unique way to experience Perisher after dark. Ski under the stars, enjoy the live music, and catch the 7:00pm fireworks! There's plenty on this week, including further demo days, live music, Station gigs , Christmas in July and more, so check the Events page or follow Perisher's socials to stay across it all! A cool change is on the way too, so get out there and make the most of the last of the bright, sunny conditions!

latest.narrative: Another sunny day forecast with a light overnight freeze underfoot.
warnings: Widget update time is a source-refresh time, not a physical snow-patrol measurement timestamp.

latest.narrative: Make the most of today’s sunshine!. Tuesday's shaping up as another mostly sunny day at Perisher, with the temperature reaching a high of 9 degrees and light westerly winds topping out at 17km/h. Although temperatures are running slightly above normal, low humidity overnight let our snowmaking team get to work, with 89 guns running through the early evening to top up the base. Expect the same firm, fast conditions underfoot as yesterday. 29 lifts are expected to spin again today, but as always, keep an eye on the lift report for real-time updates, or download the Perisher App to stay in the loop. A warm welcome to all our guests competing in the ACT and Regional NSW Interschools this week! To our other guests: please note racing will be taking place on Front and Centre Valley, so it might be worth starting your days at other resort areas like Smiggins or Blue Cow. The Ski and Board Demo Days continue on Front Valley, with Burton, Nitro, Jones, Rossignol, Head, and Elan all in-resort showcasing their latest gear! If you're in the market for new equipment, be sure to say hello and put it to the test! Night Skiing and Boarding is also on tonight from 6:00-8:30pm, offering a unique way to experience Perisher after dark. Ski under the stars, enjoy the live music, and catch the 7:00pm fireworks! There's plenty on this week, including further demo days, live music, Station gigs , Christmas in July and more, so check the Events page or follow Perisher's socials to stay across it all! A cool change is on the way too, so get out there and make the most of the last of the bright, sunny conditions!

latest.narrative: It'll be another cracker of a day on the hill!  The stars are shining and the snowguns are pumping it out thanks to the very low humidity all night.  The current temp is 2.7 degrees, heading for a top of 5 degrees and the wind is a moderate northerly which may increase later in the day.   I expect the snowmaking will make for good turns on the groomers this morning before some zones get scraped back to the hard base underneath.  The sunniest of aspects may soften for spring like turns as the day warms but the shaded and south facing slopes will probably stay very firm and slick for the day.   Heavenly Valley lift will be spinning once the patrol have completed their assessments, but the area is ONLY suitable for ADVANCED and EXPERT skiers and riders.  The cover is thin and the surface will likely remain icy on most runs for the day. The Extreme Area is Closed for ice hazard.  And DO NOT ATTEMPT to retrieve dropped equipment from any closed or out of bounds areas.  There is a dangerous sliding hazard out there at the moment. Slap on the screen, stick to the open areas and have a beaut day in the sun.  Cheers Bill

Whether you’re hitting the slopes, exploring the village or enjoying tobogganing at Windy Corner with Everest Sports, there’s plenty to experience this week. We have a great lineup of events and activations, including Twilight Tuesdays, Thursday night fireworks, live music and après experiences throughout the resort. Planning to go tobogganing? Book your Windy Corner session with Everest Sports here Discover what’s happening across the village. Explore the What’s On page HERE
